---
output:
  beamer_presentation:
    theme: "default"
    slide_level: 1
    keep_tex: false
    latex_engine: pdflatex
    includes:
      in_header: carousel_preamble.tex
classoption: "aspectratio=11"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
```

#
\begin{tikzpicture}[remember picture, overlay]
\fill[primary] (current page.north west) rectangle ([yshift=-2.5cm]current page.north east);
\fill[accent] ([yshift=-2.5cm]current page.north west) rectangle ([yshift=-2.65cm]current page.north east);
\end{tikzpicture}

\vspace{-0.5cm}
\begin{center}
\textcolor{white}{\fontsize{28}{32}\selectfont\bfseries huggingfaceR}\\[0.15cm]
\textcolor{hfyellow}{\large\bfseries 2.0}
\end{center}

\vspace{1cm}
\begin{center}
\accentline\\[0.5cm]
{\fontsize{18}{22}\selectfont\bfseries\textcolor{primary}{ML Models from the Hub}}\\[0.15cm]
{\fontsize{18}{22}\selectfont\bfseries\textcolor{primary}{Directly in R}}\\[0.7cm]
\colorbox{accentlight}{\textcolor{accent}{\bfseries\hspace{10pt}No Python Required\hspace{10pt}}}\\[1cm]
\textcolor{lighttext}{\small API-First \quad$\bullet$\quad Tidyverse-Native \quad$\bullet$\quad Open Source}
\end{center}

#
\vspace{0.2cm}
\slidetitle{What Changed in V2?}\\[0.05cm]
\accentline

\vspace{0.5cm}
\begin{tcolorbox}[colback=gray!8, colframe=gray!8, arc=4pt, boxrule=0pt, left=12pt, right=12pt, top=8pt, bottom=8pt]
\textcolor{lighttext}{\small\bfseries VERSION 1}\\[0.2cm]
\begin{tabular}{@{}l@{\hspace{10pt}}l@{}}
\textcolor{lighttext}{\faIcon{python}} & Required Python installation\\[4pt]
\textcolor{lighttext}{\faIcon{link}} & Bridged via reticulate\\[4pt]
\textcolor{lighttext}{\faIcon{cogs}} & Complex environment setup
\end{tabular}
\end{tcolorbox}

\vspace{0.25cm}
\begin{tcolorbox}[colback=accentlight, colframe=accentlight, arc=4pt, boxrule=0pt, left=12pt, right=12pt, top=8pt, bottom=8pt]
\textcolor{accent}{\small\bfseries VERSION 2.0}\\[0.2cm]
\begin{tabular}{@{}l@{\hspace{10pt}}l@{}}
\textcolor{accent}{\faIcon{check-circle}} & \textbf{Pure R} --- no Python needed\\[4pt]
\textcolor{accent}{\faIcon{check-circle}} & Native HTTP API via httr2\\[4pt]
\textcolor{accent}{\faIcon{check-circle}} & Set your token and go
\end{tabular}
\end{tcolorbox}

#
\vspace{0.2cm}
\slidetitle{What Can You Do?}\\[0.05cm]
\accentline

\vspace{0.45cm}
\begin{minipage}[t]{0.47\textwidth}
\feat{comment-dots}{Text Classification}\\[0.1cm]
\textcolor{lighttext}{\small\RaggedRight Sentiment analysis and zero-shot classification with custom labels}

\vspace{0.4cm}
\feat{project-diagram}{Embeddings}\\[0.1cm]
\textcolor{lighttext}{\small\RaggedRight Semantic similarity, clustering, and nearest neighbors}
\end{minipage}
\hfill
\begin{minipage}[t]{0.47\textwidth}
\feat{robot}{Chat with LLMs}\\[0.1cm]
\textcolor{lighttext}{\small\RaggedRight Multi-turn conversations with open-source models}

\vspace{0.4cm}
\feat{database}{Hub \& Datasets}\\[0.1cm]
\textcolor{lighttext}{\small\RaggedRight Search models and load HF datasets as tibbles}
\end{minipage}

\vspace{0.5cm}
\begin{center}
\begin{tcolorbox}[colback=codebg, colframe=codebg, arc=3pt, boxrule=0pt, left=6pt, right=6pt, top=4pt, bottom=4pt, width=0.95\textwidth]
\centering\small\ttfamily hf\_classify() \quad hf\_embed() \quad hf\_chat() \quad hf\_load\_dataset()
\end{tcolorbox}
\end{center}

#
\vspace{0.2cm}
\slidetitle{Tidyverse Native}\\[0.05cm]
\accentline

\vspace{0.3cm}
\textcolor{lighttext}{\small Everything returns tibbles. Works seamlessly in pipes.}

\vspace{0.35cm}
\begin{tcolorbox}[colback=codebg, colframe=codebg, arc=3pt, boxrule=0pt, left=10pt, right=10pt, top=8pt, bottom=8pt]
\ttfamily\small
\textcolor{accent}{\#}~Classify~text~in~a~pipeline\\
reviews~|>\\
~~mutate(sentiment~=~hf\_classify(text))~|>\\
~~unnest(sentiment)
\end{tcolorbox}

\vspace{0.25cm}
\begin{tcolorbox}[colback=codebg, colframe=codebg, arc=3pt, boxrule=0pt, left=10pt, right=10pt, top=8pt, bottom=8pt]
\ttfamily\small
\textcolor{accent}{\#}~Generate~embeddings\\
docs~|>~hf\_embed(text)\\[4pt]
\textcolor{accent}{\#}~Compute~pairwise~similarity\\
embeddings~|>~hf\_similarity()
\end{tcolorbox}

#
\vspace{0.2cm}
\slidetitle{Tidymodels Integration}\\[0.05cm]
\accentline

\vspace{0.3cm}
\textcolor{lighttext}{\small Use embeddings as features in machine learning workflows.}

\vspace{0.35cm}
\begin{tcolorbox}[colback=codebg, colframe=codebg, arc=3pt, boxrule=0pt, left=10pt, right=10pt, top=8pt, bottom=8pt]
\ttfamily\small
library(tidymodels)\\[4pt]
recipe(outcome~\textasciitilde{}~text,~data~=~train)~|>\\
~~step\_hf\_embed(text)~|>\\
~~prep()
\end{tcolorbox}

\vspace{0.35cm}
\begin{minipage}[t]{0.47\textwidth}
\textcolor{accent}{\faIcon{cube}}\hspace{6pt}\textcolor{darktext}{\bfseries\small Recipe Step}\\[0.1cm]
\textcolor{lighttext}{\footnotesize\RaggedRight \texttt{step\_hf\_embed()} converts text columns to dense embedding vectors}
\end{minipage}
\hfill
\begin{minipage}[t]{0.47\textwidth}
\textcolor{accent}{\faIcon{random}}\hspace{6pt}\textcolor{darktext}{\bfseries\small Any Workflow}\\[0.1cm]
\textcolor{lighttext}{\footnotesize\RaggedRight Plug into classification, regression, or clustering pipelines}
\end{minipage}

#
\begin{tikzpicture}[remember picture, overlay]
\fill[primary] (current page.south west) rectangle ([yshift=1.8cm]current page.south east);
\end{tikzpicture}

\vspace{0.2cm}
\slidetitle{Get Started}\\[0.05cm]
\accentline

\vspace{0.4cm}
\begin{tcolorbox}[colback=codebg, colframe=codebg, arc=3pt, boxrule=0pt, left=10pt, right=10pt, top=8pt, bottom=8pt]
\ttfamily\small devtools::install\_github("farach/huggingfaceR")
\end{tcolorbox}

\vspace{0.35cm}
\textcolor{accent}{\faIcon{globe}}\hspace{8pt}\textcolor{darktext}{\bfseries farach.github.io/huggingfaceR}\\[0.2cm]
\textcolor{accent}{\faIcon{book}}\hspace{8pt}\textcolor{darktext}{\bfseries OpenAI GDPval Benchmark Vignette}\\[0.2cm]
\textcolor{accent}{\faIcon{github}}\hspace{8pt}\textcolor{darktext}{\bfseries github.com/farach/huggingfaceR}

\vspace{0.5cm}
\begin{center}
\textcolor{lighttext}{\small Built on the foundation created with}\\[0.1cm]
\textcolor{darktext}{\bfseries Sam Terfa}~\textcolor{lighttext}{\&}~\textcolor{darktext}{\bfseries Jack Penzer}
\end{center}
